author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 19, 20.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What in the holy Gospel is spoken briefly ought not briefly to be expounded, so that what is read may be understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The words of the Lord are few, but great; to be valued not by number, but by weight: not to be despised because they are few, but to be sought because they are great.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You who were present yesterday have heard, as we discoursed according to our ability from that which the Lord said, “Ye judge after the flesh: I judge not any man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet if I judge, my judgment is true; because I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is written in your law, that the testimony of two men is true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am one that bear witness of myself, and the Father that sent me beareth witness of me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yesterday, as I have said, from these words a discourse was delivered to your ears and to your minds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord had spoken these words, they who heard, “Ye judge after the flesh,” manifested the truth of what they had heard.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they answered the Lord, as He spoke of God His Father, and said to Him, “Where is thy Father?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father of Christ they understood carnally, because they judged the words of Christ after the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He who spoke was openly flesh, but secretly the Word: man visible, God hidden.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They saw the covering, and despised the wearer: they despised because they knew not; knew not, because they saw not; saw not, because they were blind; they were blind, because they believed not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see, then, what answer the Lord made to this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Where,” say they, “is thy Father?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we have heard thee say, “I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me:” we see thee alone, we do not see thy Father with thee; how sayest thou that thou art not alone, but that thou art with thy Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Else show us that thy Father is with thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord answered them: Do ye know me, that I should show you the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is indeed what follows; this is what He answered in His own words, the exposition of which we have already premised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For see what He said, “Ye neither know me nor my Father: if ye knew me, ye would perhaps know my Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye say then, “Where is thy Father?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if already ye knew me; as if what you see were all that I am.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore because ye know not me, I do not show you my Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye suppose me, in fact, to be a man; hence ye seek a man for my father, because “ye judge after the flesh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because, according to what you see, I am one thing, and another thing according to what you see not, and that I as hidden from you speak of my Father as hidden, it is requisite that you should first know me, and then ye know my Father also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For if ye knew me, ye would perhaps know my Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who knows all things is not in doubt when He says perhaps, but rebuking.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now see how this very word perhaps, which seems to be a word of doubting, may be spoken chidingly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yea, a word expressive of doubt it is when used by man, for man doubts because he knows not; but when a word of doubting is spoken by God, from whom surely nothing is hid, it is unbelief that is reproved by that doubting, not the Godhead merely expressing an opinion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For men sometimes chidingly express doubt concerning things which they hold certain; that is, use a word of doubting, while in their heart they doubt not: just as thou wouldst say to thy slave, if thou wert angry with him, “Thou despisest me; but consider, perhaps I am thy master.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence also the apostle, speaking to some who despised him, says: “And I think that I also have the Spirit of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When he says, “I think,” he seems to doubt; but he is rebuking, not doubting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And in another place the Lord Jesus Christ Himself, rebuking the future unbelief of mankind, saith: “When the Son of man cometh, will He, thinkest thou, find faith on the earth?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You now, as I think, understand how the word perhaps is used here, in case any weigher of words and poiser of syllables, as if to show his knowledge of Latin, finds fault with a word which the Word of God spoke; and by blaming the Word of God, remain not eloquent, but mute.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who is there that speaks as doth the Word which was in the beginning with God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not consider these words as we use them, and from these wish to measure that Word which is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hearest the Word indeed, and despisest it; hear God and fear Him: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou referrest to the usage of thy conversation, and sayest within thyself, What is a word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What mighty thing is a word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It sounds and passes away; after beating the air, it strikes the ear and is no more.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear further: “The Word was with God;” remained, did not by sounding pass away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps thou still despisest it: “The Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With thyself, O man, a word in thy heart is a different thing from sound; but the word that is with thee, in order to pass to me, requires sound for a vehicle as it were.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It takes to itself sound, mounts it as a vehicle, runs through the air, comes to me and yet does not leave thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the sound, in order to come to me, left thee and yet did not stay with me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now has the word that was in thy heart also passed away with the passing sound?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou didst speak thy thought; and, that the thought which was hid with thee might come to me, thou didst sound syllables; the sound of the syllables conveyed thy thought to my ear; through my ear thy thought descended into my heart, the intermediate sound flew away: but that word which took to itself sound was with thee before thou didst sound it, and is with me, because thou didst sound it, without quitting thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider this, thou nice weigher of sounds, whoever thou be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou despisest the Word of God, thou who comprehendest not the word of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, then, by whom all things were made knows all things, and yet He rebukes by doubting: “If ye knew me ye would perhaps know my Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He rebukes unbelievers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He spoke a like sentence to the disciples, but there is not a word of doubting in it, because there was no occasion to rebuke unbelief.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this, “If ye knew me, ye would perhaps know my Father also,” which He said to the Jews, He said also to the disciples, when Philip asked, or rather, demanded of Him, saying, “Lord, show us the Father, and it sufficeth us:” just as if he said, We already know Thee even ourselves; Thou hast been apparent to us; we have seen Thee; Thou hast deigned to choose us; we have followed Thee, have seen Thy marvels, heard Thy words of Salvation, have taken Thy precepts upon us, we hope in Thy promises: Thou hast deigned to confer much upon us by Thy very presence: but still, while we know Thee, and we do not yet know the Father, we are inflamed with desire to see Him whom we do not yet know; and thus, be cause we know Thee, but it is not enough until we know the Father, show us the Father and it sufficeth us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord, that they might understand that they knew not what they thought they did already know, said, “Am I so long time with you, and ye know me not, Philip? he who hath seen me hath seen the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has this sentence a word of doubting in it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did He say, He that hath seen me hath perhaps seen the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because it was a believer that listened to Him, not a persecutor of the faith: hence did the Lord not rebuke, but teach.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whoso hath seen me hath seen the Father also:” and here, “If ye knew me, ye would know my Father also,” let us remove the word which indicates the unbelief of the hearers, and it is the same sentence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yesterday we commended it to your consideration, beloved, and said that the sentences of the Evangelist John, in which he narrates to us what he learned from the Lord, had not required to be discussed, were that possible, except the inventions of heretics had compelled us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yesterday, then, we briefly intimated to you, beloved, that there are heretics who are called Patripassians, or Sabellians after their founder: these say that the same is the Father who is the Son; the names different, but the person one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He wills, say they, He is Father; when He wills, He is Son: still He is one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are likewise other heretics who are called Arians.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They indeed confess that our Lord Jesus Christ is the only Son of the Father; the one, Father of the Son; the other, Son of the Father; that He who is Father is not Son, nor He who is Son is Father; they confess that the Son was begotten, but deny His equality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We, namely, the catholic faith, coming from the doctrine of the apostles planted in us, received by a line of succession, to be transmitted sound to posterity,—the catholic faith, I say, has, between both those parties, that is, between both errors, held the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the error of the Sabellians, He is only one; the Father and Son is the same person: in the error of the Arians, the Father and the Son are indeed different persons; but the Son is not only a different person, but different in nature.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou midway between these, what sayest thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast shut out the Sabellian, shut out the Arian also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father is Father, the Son is Son; another person, not another in nature; for, “I and the Father are one,” which, so far as I could, I pressed on your thoughts yesterday.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When he hears that word, we are, let the Sabellian go away confounded; when he hears the word one, let the Arian go away confounded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the catholic steer the bark of his faith between both, since in both he must be on his guard against shipwreck.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Say thou, then, what the Gospel saith, “I and the Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not different in nature, because one; not one person, because are.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A little before He said, “My judgment is true; because I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent me:” as if He said, The reason why my judgment is true is, because I am the Son of God, because I speak the truth, because I am truth itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those men, understanding Him carnally, said, “Where is thy Father?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now hear, O Arian: “Ye neither know me, nor my Father;” because, “If ye knew me, ye would know my Father also.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What doth this mean, except “I and the Father are one”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou seest some person like some other,—give heed, beloved, it is a common remark; let not that appear to you difficult which you see to be customary,—when, I say, thou seest some person like another, and thou knowest the person to whom he is like, thou sayest in wonder, “How like this person is to that!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wouldst not say this unless there were two.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here one who does not know the person to whom thou sayest the other is like remarks, “Is he so like him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thou answerest him: What, dost thou not know that person?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Saith he, “No, I do not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Immediately thou, in order to make known to him the person whom he does not know by means of the person whom he observes before him, answerest, saying, Having seen this man, thou hast seen the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou didst not, surely, assert that they are one person in saying this, or that they are not two; but made such answer because of the likeness: “If thou knowest the one, thou knowest the other; for they are very like, and there is no difference whatever between them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence also the Lord saith, “If ye knew me, ye would know my Father also;” not that the Son is the Father but like the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the Arian blush.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thanks be to the Lord that even the Arian is separate from the Sabellian error, and is not a Patripassian: he does not affirm that the Father assumed flesh and came to men, that the Father suffered, rose again, and somehow ascended to Himself; this he does not affirm; he acknowledges with me the Father to be Father, the Son to be Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, O brother, thou hast escaped that shipwreck, why go to the other?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Father is Father, Son is Son; why dost thou affirm that the Son is unlike, that He is different, another substance?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He were unlike, would He say to His disciples, “He that hath seen me hath seen the Father”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would He say to the Jews, “If ye knew me, ye would know my Father also”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How would this be true, unless that other was also true, “I and the Father are one”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “These words spake Jesus in the treasury, speaking in the temple:” great boldness, without fear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He could not suffer if He did not will it, since He were not born if He did not will it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What follows then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And no man laid hold of Him, because His hour was not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some, again, when they hear this, believe that the Lord Christ was subject to fate, and say: Behold, Christ is held by fate!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O, if thy heart were not fatuous, thou wouldst not believe in fate.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If fate, as some understand it, is derived from fando, that is from speaking, how can the Word of God be held by fate, whilst all things that are made are in the Word itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God has not ordained anything which He did not know beforehand; that which was made was in His Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The world was made; both was made and was there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How both was made and was there?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the house which the builder rears, was previously in his art; and there, a better house, without age, without decay: however, to show forth his art, he makes a house; and so, in a manner, a house comes forth from a house; and if the house should fall, the art remains.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So were all things that are made with the Word of God; because God made all things in wisdom, and all that He made were known to Him: for He did not learn because He made, but made because He knew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To us they are known, because they are made: to Him, if they had not been known, they would not have been made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the Word went before.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what was before the Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nothing at all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For were there anything before it, it would not have been said, “In the beginning was the Word;” but, In the beginning was the Word made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, what says Moses concerning the world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning God made the heavens and the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Made what was not: well, if He made what was not, what was there before?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence came heaven and earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “All things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou then put Christ under fate?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where are the fates?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In heaven, sayest thou, in the order and changes of the stars.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then can fate rule Him by whom the heavens and the stars were made; whilst thy own will, if thou thinkest rightly, transcends even the stars?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, because thou knowest that Christ’s flesh was under heaven, is that the reason why thou thinkest that Christ’s power was put under the heavens?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear, thou fool: “His hour was not yet come;” not the hour in which He should be forced to die, but that in which He would deign to be put to death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Himself knew when He should die: He considered all things that were foretold of Him, and awaited all to be finished that was foretold to be before His suffering; that when all should be fulfilled, then should come His suffering in set order, not by fatal necessity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, hear that you may prove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among the rest that was prophesied of Him, it is also written: “They gave me gall for meat, and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How this happened, we know from the Gospel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: First, they gave Him gall; He received it, tasted it, and spat it out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thereafter, as He hung on the cross, that all that was foretold might be fulfilled, He said, “I thirst.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They took a sponge filled with vinegar, bound it to a reed, and put it to His mouth; He received it, and said, “It is finished.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did that mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All things which were prophesied before my death are completed, then what do I here any longer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a word, when He said “It is finished, He bowed His head, and gave up the ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did the thieves, who were nailed beside Him, expire when they would?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were held by the bonds of flesh, for they were not the creators of the flesh; fixed by nails, they were a long time tormented, because they had not lordship over their weakness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, however, when He would, took flesh in a virgin’s womb: came forth to men when He would; lived among men so long as He would; and when He would He quitted the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the part of power, not of necessity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This hour, then, He awaited; not the fated, but the fitting and voluntary hour; that all might first be fulfilled which behoved to be fulfilled before His decease.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How could he have been under necessity of fate, when He said in another place, “I have power to lay down my life, and I have power to take it again: no man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself and take it again?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He showed this power when the Jews sought Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whom seek ye?” saith He.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus,” said they.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He answered, “I am He.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When they heard this voice, “they went back and fell to the ground.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Says one, If he had this power, why, when the Jews insulted him on the cross and said, “If he be the Son of God let him come down from the cross,” did He not come down, to show them His power by coming down?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because He was teaching us patience, therefore He deferred the demonstration of His power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He came down, moved as it were at their words, He would be thought to have been overcome by the sting of their insults.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not come down; there He remained fixed, to depart when He would.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what great matter was it for Him to descend from the cross, when He could rise again from the sepulchre?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us, then, to whom this is ministered, understand that the power of our Lord Jesus Christ, then concealed, will be made manifest in the judgment, of which it is said, “God will come manifest; our God, and He will not be silent.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why is it said, “will come manifest”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because He, our God,—namely, Christ,—came hidden, will come manifest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And will not be silent:” why this “will not be silent”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because at first He did keep silence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He was judged; that this, too, might be fulfilled which the prophet had foretold: “As a sheep He was led to the slaughter, and as a lamb before his shearer is dumb, so He opened not His mouth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He would not have suffered did He not will to suffer: did He not suffer, that blood had not been shed; if that blood were not shed, the world would not be redeemed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let us give thanks to the power of His divinity, and to the compassion of His infirmity; both concerning the hidden power which the Jews did not recognize, whence it is now said to them, “Ye neither know me nor my Father,” and also concerning the flesh assumed, which the Jews did not recognize, and yet knew His lineage: whence He said to them elsewhere, “Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us know both in Christ, both wherein He is equal to the Father and wherein the Father is greater than He.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is the Word, this is the flesh; that is God, this is man; but yet Christ is one, God and man.
